pi_items.source: Python rasterization script simulating setup_task.sh logic on the actual downloaded Landsat TIFF, yielding 0.32 area ratio
Python calculation confirming that restricting the 32% area diamond to its bounding box (64% area) yields a valid pixel ratio of 0.50
Confirmed via export_result.sh which explicitly globs '/home/ga/snap_exports/*.hdr' to find the result

privileged_info_summary: The base dataset is a real Landsat multi-spectral image downloaded from opengeos, while the shapefile used as a spatial mask is dynamically generated during setup. It forms a diamond shape covering exactly 32% of the full image. If the image is cropped to the polygon's bounding box during masking, the valid pixel ratio becomes exactly 50%. The target export directory /home/ga/snap_exports/ is explicitly checked by the verification scripts.
